lesson.title: Lesson 45: The Power of the Press

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: Does the writer think the parents were lucky or unlucky to gain prosperity in this way?

translation.text: 作者认为这对父母以这种方式变得富裕，是幸运还是不幸？

segments.text: Why?

translation.text: 为什么？

segments.text: In democratic countries, any efforts to restrict the freedom of the press are rightly condemned.

translation.text: 在民主国家，任何限制新闻自由的企图都理应受到谴责。

segments.text: However, this freedom can easily be abused.

translation.text: 然而，这种自由很容易被滥用。

segments.text: Stories about people often attract far more public attention than political events.

translation.text: 关于个人的报道往往比政治事件更能吸引公众注意。

segments.text: Though we may enjoy reading about the lives of others, it is extremely doubtful whether we would equally enjoy reading about ourselves.

translation.text: 我们也许喜欢读别人的生活故事，但是否同样喜欢读有关自己的报道，就极其可疑了。

segments.text: Acting on the contention that facts are sacred, can cause untold suffering to individuals by publishing details about their private lives.

translation.text: 有人以“事实神圣不可侵犯”为由，刊登个人私生活的细节，给当事人造成无法估量的痛苦。

segments.text: Newspapers exert such tremendous influence that they can not only bring about major changes to the lives of ordinary people, but can even overthrow a government.

translation.text: 报纸影响力极大，不仅能使普通人的生活发生重大改变，甚至能推翻一个政府。

segments.text: The story of a poor family that acquired fame and fortune overnight dramatically illustrates the power of the press.

translation.text: 一个贫困家庭一夜之间名利双收的故事，生动说明了新闻界的力量。

segments.text: The family lived in Aberdeen, a small town of 23,000 inhabitants in South Dakota.

translation.text: 这家人住在南达科他州一座拥有2.3万居民的小城阿伯丁。

segments.text: As the parents had five children, life was a perpetual struggle against poverty.

translation.text: 父母已有五个孩子，生活就是一场没完没了的反贫困斗争。

segments.text: They were expecting their sixth child and were faced with even more pressing economic problems.

translation.text: 他们正期待第六个孩子，面临更加紧迫的经济问题。

segments.text: If they had only had one more child, the fact would have passed unnoticed.

translation.text: 如果他们只是又多了一个孩子，这件事根本不会引人注意。

segments.text: They would have continued to struggle against economic odds and would have lived in obscurity.

translation.text: 他们会继续在经济困境中挣扎，默默无闻地生活。

segments.text: But they suddenly became the parents of quintuplets, four girls and a boy, an event which radically changed their lives.

translation.text: 然而他们突然成了五胞胎——四女一男——的父母，这件事彻底改变了他们的生活。

segments.text: The day after the birth of the five children, an airplane arrived in Aberdeen, bringing sixty reporters and photographers.

translation.text: 五个孩子出生后的第二天，一架飞机抵达阿伯丁，带来了60名记者和摄影师。

segments.text: The rise to fame was swift.

translation.text: 他们迅速出了名。

segments.text: Television cameras and newspapers carried the news to everyone in the country.

translation.text: 电视镜头和报纸把消息传遍全国。

segments.text: Newspapers and magazines offered the family huge sums for the exclusive rights to publish stories and photographs.

translation.text: 报刊向这家人开出巨额报酬，购买独家刊登报道和照片的权利。

segments.text: poured in not only from unknown people, but from baby food and soap manufacturers who wished to advertise their products.

translation.text: 礼物纷纷涌来，不仅来自素不相识的人，也来自希望宣传产品的婴儿食品和肥皂制造商。

segments.text: The old farmhouse the family lived in was to be replaced by a new, five hundred thousand dollar home.

translation.text: 这家人居住的旧农舍将被一幢价值50万美元的新房取代。

segments.text: Reporters kept pressing for interviews, so lawyers had to be employed to act as spokesmen for the family at press conferences.

translation.text: 记者不断要求采访，因此不得不雇用律师，在新闻发布会上担任这家人的发言人。

segments.text: While the five babies were still quietly sleeping in oxygen tents in a hospital nursery, their parents were paying the price for fame.

translation.text: 五个婴儿还在医院育婴室的氧气帐里安静睡觉时，他们的父母已经在为成名付出代价。

segments.text: It would never again be possible for them to lead normal lives.

translation.text: 他们再也不可能过正常生活了。

segments.text: They had become the victims of commercialization, for their names had acquired a market value.

translation.text: 他们成了商业化的受害者，因为他们的姓名已经有了市场价值。

segments.text: Instead of being five new family members, these children had immediately become a commodity.

translation.text: 这些孩子不再只是五名新的家庭成员，而是立刻变成了商品。
